0.label: receives
0.description: An AI system takes in input data to generate outputs.

1.label: uses
1.description: Emotion recognition systems, biometric categorisation systems, and remote biometric identification systems use biometric data for their operations.

2.label: isA
2.description: A general-purpose AI system is a specific type of AI system.

3.label: isA
3.description: An emotion recognition system is a specific type of AI system.

4.label: isA
4.description: A biometric categorisation system is a specific type of AI system.

5.label: isA
5.description: A remote biometric identification system is a specific type of AI system.

6.label: isA
6.description: A real-time remote biometric identification system is a specific type of remote biometric identification system.

7.label: isA
7.description: A post-remote biometric identification system is a specific type of remote biometric identification system.

8.label: poses
8.description: An AI system can pose risks to health, safety, or fundamental rights.

9.label: isUsedAs
9.description: An AI system can be intended for use as a safety component of a product.

10.label: accompaniedBy
10.description: High-risk AI systems are accompanied by instructions for use.

11.label: canBe
11.description: An AI system's placing on the market, putting into service, or use can constitute a prohibited AI practice.

12.label: canBe
12.description: An AI system can be understood to present a risk to health, safety, or fundamental rights, leading to this status.

13.label: generates
13.description: An AI system, specifically a synthetic content AI system, can generate or manipulate content constituting a deep fake.

14.label: trainedWith
14.description: AI systems making use of training techniques are developed based on training data.

15.label: evaluatedWith
15.description: AI systems are evaluated using validation data.

16.label: evaluatedWith
16.description: AI systems are evaluated using testing data.

17.label: canBeIntegratedInto
17.description: A general-purpose AI model can be integrated into a variety of downstream AI systems or applications.

18.label: canBeClassifiedAs
18.description: A general-purpose AI model can be classified as having systemic risk if it meets certain conditions.

19.label: possesses
19.description: A general-purpose AI model classified with systemic risk possesses high-impact capabilities.

20.label: isA
20.description: Biometric data is a type of personal data.

21.label: isA
21.description: Special categories of personal data are a subset of personal data.

22.label: isOtherThan
22.description: Non-personal data is distinct from personal data.

23.label: isA
23.description: Systemic risk is a specific type of risk, typically associated with high-impact capabilities of general-purpose AI models.

24.label: isA
24.description: A provider is defined as a type of operator.

25.label: isA
25.description: A deployer is defined as a type of operator.

26.label: isA
26.description: An authorised representative is defined as a type of operator.

27.label: isA
27.description: An importer is defined as a type of operator.

28.label: isA
28.description: A distributor is defined as a type of operator.

29.label: includes
29.description: A national competent authority includes a notifying authority.

30.label: includes
30.description: A national competent authority includes a market surveillance authority.

31.label: isA
31.description: A notified body is a type of conformity assessment body that has been officially notified.

32.label: develops
32.description: A provider develops an AI system.

33.label: develops
33.description: A provider develops a general-purpose AI model.

34.label: placesOnTheMarket
34.description: A provider places an AI system on the market.

35.label: placesOnTheMarket
35.description: A provider places a general-purpose AI model on the market.

36.label: putsIntoService
36.description: A provider puts an AI system into service.

37.label: provides
37.description: A provider provides instructions for use for an AI system.

38.label: establishes
38.description: A provider establishes a post-market monitoring system for high-risk AI systems.

39.label: ensuresComplianceOf
39.description: Providers ensure their high-risk AI systems are compliant with requirements.

40.label: ensuresUndergoes
40.description: Providers ensure their high-risk AI system undergoes conformity assessment.

41.label: affixes
41.description: A provider affixes the CE marking to an AI system.

42.label: demonstratesConformityTo
42.description: Providers demonstrate AI system conformity to national competent authorities upon request.

43.label: keepsAtDisposalOf
43.description: Providers keep documentation (e.g., technical documentation, quality management system documentation) at the disposal of national competent authorities.

44.label: informs
44.description: Providers inform distributors of AI system non-conformity or risks.

45.label: informs
45.description: Providers inform deployers of AI system non-conformity or risks.

46.label: informs
46.description: Providers inform authorised representatives of AI system non-conformity or risks.

47.label: informs
47.description: Providers inform importers of AI system non-conformity or risks.

48.label: takesCorrectiveActionsFor
48.description: Providers take corrective actions for non-compliant AI systems.

49.label: initiates
49.description: Providers initiate the withdrawal of non-compliant AI systems from the market.

50.label: initiates
50.description: Providers initiate the recall of non-compliant AI systems.

51.label: informs
51.description: Providers inform market surveillance authorities of AI system risks or non-compliance.

52.label: informs
52.description: Providers inform notified bodies of AI system risks or non-compliance.

53.label: grantsAccessTo
53.description: Providers grant access to AI system logs to national competent authorities upon request.

54.label: appoints
54.description: Providers established in third countries appoint an authorised representative in the Union.

55.label: mandates
55.description: A provider gives a written mandate to its authorised representative to perform obligations.

56.label: cooperatesWith
56.description: An initial provider cooperates with a new deemed provider for AI system compliance.

57.label: providesAssistanceTo
57.description: An initial provider provides necessary information and technical assistance to a new deemed provider for AI system compliance.

58.label: contractsWith
58.description: A provider of a high-risk AI system contracts with a third party that supplies components, tools, or services.

59.label: informs
59.description: Providers inform affected persons of direct interaction with an AI system.

60.label: marksOutputsOf
60.description: Providers of synthetic content AI systems mark their outputs as artificially generated or manipulated.

61.label: notifies
61.description: Providers of general-purpose AI models notify the AI Office if their model meets systemic risk conditions.

62.label: drawsUpDocumentationFor
62.description: Providers draw up technical documentation for their general-purpose AI models.

63.label: providesDocumentationTo
63.description: Providers provide technical documentation for general-purpose AI models to the AI Office upon request.

64.label: providesDocumentationTo
64.description: Providers provide technical documentation for general-purpose AI models to national competent authorities upon request.

65.label: providesInformationTo
65.description: Providers of general-purpose AI models make information available to downstream providers for integration.

66.label: has
66.description: Providers of general-purpose AI models have an obligation to implement a policy for compliance with Union copyright law.

67.label: makesAvailableSummaryOf
67.description: Providers of general-purpose AI models make a sufficiently detailed summary about the content used for training publicly available.

68.label: evaluates
68.description: Providers of systemic risk general-purpose AI models perform model evaluations to identify and mitigate systemic risks.

69.label: mitigates
69.description: Providers of systemic risk general-purpose AI models assess and mitigate possible systemic risks.

70.label: reports
70.description: Providers of systemic risk general-purpose AI models report relevant information about serious incidents.

71.label: ensuresCybersecurityFor
71.description: Providers ensure an adequate level of cybersecurity protection for systemic risk general-purpose AI models.

72.label: conducts
72.description: Providers may conduct testing of high-risk AI systems in real-world conditions.

73.label: submits
73.description: Providers submit a real-world testing plan to the market surveillance authority.

74.label: registers
74.description: Providers register testing in real-world conditions in the EU database.

75.label: cooperatesWith
75.description: Providers cooperate with deployers when organizing testing in real-world conditions.

76.label: isExemptFrom
76.description: Providers participating in an AI regulatory sandbox under certain conditions are exempt from administrative fines for infringements of the Regulation.

77.label: isExemptFrom
77.description: Providers participating in an AI regulatory sandbox under certain conditions are exempt from administrative fines for infringements of the Regulation.

78.label: triggers
78.description: Non-compliance with prohibited AI practices by a provider triggers administrative fines.

79.label: triggers
79.description: Non-compliance with obligations by a provider (other than prohibited practices) triggers administrative fines.

80.label: isSubjectTo
80.description: Providers of general-purpose AI models are subject to administrative fines for non-compliance with their obligations.

81.label: failsToAffix
81.description: A provider's failure to affix the CE marking leads to formal non-compliance.

82.label: failsToAppoint
82.description: A provider's failure to appoint an authorised representative leads to formal non-compliance.

83.label: uses
83.description: A deployer uses an AI system under its authority.

84.label: reportsTo
84.description: Deployers report serious incidents or risks related to AI systems to providers.

85.label: informs
85.description: Deployers inform market surveillance authorities of AI system risks or serious incidents.

86.label: informs
86.description: Deployers inform importers of serious incidents related to AI systems.

87.label: receives
87.description: Deployers receive instructions for use for an AI system.

88.label: informs
88.description: Deployers inform affected persons of AI system use in the workplace or in decision-making.

89.label: informs
89.description: Deployers inform distributors of unregistered AI systems.

90.label: requestsAuthorizationFrom
90.description: Deployers of post-remote biometric identification systems request authorization from law enforcement authorities for system use.

91.label: submitsReportsTo
91.description: Deployers submit annual reports on post-remote biometric identification system use to market surveillance authorities.

92.label: submitsReportsTo
92.description: Deployers submit annual reports on post-remote biometric identification system use to national competent authorities.

93.label: performs
93.description: Public service deployers perform a fundamental rights impact assessment for high-risk AI systems.

94.label: notifies
94.description: Deployers notify market surveillance authorities of fundamental rights impact assessment results.

95.label: represents
95.description: An authorised representative represents a provider and acts on its behalf.

96.label: actsForProviderOf
96.description: An authorised representative acts on behalf of a provider of an AI system.

97.label: actsForProviderOf
97.description: An authorised representative acts on behalf of a provider of a general-purpose AI model.

98.label: verifies
98.description: An authorised representative verifies that the provider has carried out the conformity assessment.

99.label: keepsAtDisposalOf
99.description: An authorised representative keeps documentation at the disposal of national competent authorities.

100.label: providesInformationTo
100.description: An authorised representative provides information to national competent authorities for AI system conformity.

101.label: cooperatesWith
101.description: An authorised representative cooperates with national competent authorities to mitigate AI system risks.

102.label: registers
102.description: An authorised representative complies with registration obligations for AI systems in the EU database.

103.label: informs
103.description: An authorised representative informs the market surveillance authority of mandate termination or AI system risks.

104.label: informs
104.description: An authorised representative informs the notified body of mandate termination.

105.label: keepsAtDisposalOf
105.description: An authorised representative keeps documentation for general-purpose AI models at the disposal of the AI Office.

106.label: providesInformationTo
106.description: An authorised representative provides information to the AI Office for general-purpose AI model compliance.

107.label: cooperatesWith
107.description: An authorised representative cooperates with the AI Office for general-purpose AI model compliance.

108.label: informs
108.description: An authorised representative informs the AI Office of mandate termination.

109.label: placesOnTheMarket
109.description: An importer places an AI system on the market.

110.label: verifies
110.description: Importers verify that the provider has carried out the conformity assessment for the AI system.

111.label: verifies
111.description: Importers verify that the provider has appointed an authorised representative.

112.label: informs
112.description: Importers inform providers of AI system risks or non-conformity.

113.label: informs
113.description: Importers inform authorised representatives of AI system risks or non-conformity.

114.label: informs
114.description: Importers inform market surveillance authorities of AI system risks or non-conformity.

115.label: labels
115.description: Importers label AI systems with their identification.

116.label: ensuresComplianceOf
116.description: Importers ensure AI system compliance during storage or transport.

117.label: keeps
117.description: Importers keep instructions for use for AI systems.

118.label: providesInformationTo
118.description: Importers provide information to national competent authorities for AI system conformity.

119.label: cooperatesWith
119.description: Importers cooperate with national competent authorities to mitigate AI system risks.

120.label: makesAvailableOnTheMarket
120.description: A distributor makes an AI system available on the market.

121.label: verifies
121.description: Distributors verify that the AI system bears the required CE marking.

122.label: verifies
122.description: Distributors verify that the AI system is accompanied by instructions for use.

123.label: verifiesComplianceOf
123.description: Distributors verify that the provider has complied with their obligations.

124.label: verifiesComplianceOf
124.description: Distributors verify that the importer has complied with their obligations.

125.label: informs
125.description: Distributors inform providers of AI system risks or non-conformity.

126.label: informs
126.description: Distributors inform importers of AI system risks or non-conformity.

127.label: ensuresComplianceOf
127.description: Distributors ensure AI system compliance during storage or transport.

128.label: takesCorrectiveActionsFor
128.description: Distributors take corrective actions for non-compliant AI systems.

129.label: informs
129.description: Distributors inform national competent authorities of AI system risks or non-conformity.

130.label: providesInformationTo
130.description: Distributors provide information to national competent authorities for AI system conformity.

131.label: cooperatesWith
131.description: Distributors cooperate with national competent authorities to mitigate AI system risks.

132.label: assumesObligationsOf
132.description: A deemed provider assumes the full obligations of a provider.

133.label: putsNameOrTrademarkOn
133.description: A deemed provider puts its name or trademark on an AI system, thereby assuming provider obligations.

134.label: makes
134.description: A deemed provider makes a substantial modification to an AI system.

135.label: modifies
135.description: A deemed provider modifies the intended purpose of an AI system.

136.label: supplies
136.description: An AI component supplier supplies tools, services, components, or processes for an AI system.

137.label: performs
137.description: Public service deployers perform a fundamental rights impact assessment for high-risk AI systems.

138.label: has
138.description: An affected person has the right to obtain explanations regarding decisions based on high-risk AI systems.

139.label: gives
139.description: Subjects of real-world testing give informed consent.

140.label: forParticipationIn
140.description: Real-world testing consent is given for participation in testing in real-world conditions.

141.label: isA
141.description: Real-world testing consent is a specific type of informed consent.

142.label: appliesTo
142.description: Notified bodies must maintain independence from providers and other operators with economic interest.

143.label: isIndependentOf
143.description: Notified bodies are independent of the provider of an AI system they assess.

144.label: isIndependentOf
144.description: Notified bodies are independent of any other operator having an economic interest in assessed AI systems.

145.label: notInvolvedIn
145.description: Notified bodies and their personnel are not directly involved in the design, development, marketing, or use of high-risk AI systems.

146.label: submitsApplicationTo
146.description: Conformity assessment bodies submit applications for notification to the notifying authority.

147.label: achieves
147.description: A conformity assessment body demonstrates conformity with harmonised standards to achieve this status.

148.label: basedOnConformityWith
148.description: Presumption of conformity is based on conformity with harmonised standards.

149.label: basedOnConformityWith
149.description: Presumption of conformity is based on conformity with common specifications.

150.label: appliesTo
150.description: An AI system can achieve presumption of conformity by meeting certain criteria.

151.label: appliesTo
151.description: A general-purpose AI model can achieve presumption of conformity by meeting certain criteria.

152.label: evaluates
152.description: Market surveillance authorities evaluate AI systems presenting a risk.

153.label: requiresCorrectiveActionFrom
153.description: Market surveillance authorities require operators to take corrective actions for non-compliant AI systems.

154.label: requires
154.description: Market surveillance authorities require the withdrawal of non-compliant AI systems.

155.label: requires
155.description: Market surveillance authorities require the recall of non-compliant AI systems.

156.label: prohibitsAvailabilityOf
156.description: Market surveillance authorities can prohibit or restrict an AI system from being made available on its national market or put into service.

157.label: withdraws
157.description: Market surveillance authorities can withdraw an AI system from the market.

158.label: recalls
158.description: Market surveillance authorities can recall an AI system.

159.label: evaluatesClassificationOf
159.description: Market surveillance authorities evaluate the classification of AI systems by providers (e.g., as non-high-risk).

160.label: requiresCorrectiveActionFrom
160.description: Market surveillance authorities require providers to take corrective actions for misclassified AI systems.

161.label: requiresMeasuresFrom
161.description: Market surveillance authorities require operators to take measures for compliant AI systems that still present a risk.

162.label: grants
162.description: Market surveillance authorities may authorise derogations for placing or putting into service specific high-risk AI systems for public interest reasons.

163.label: approves
163.description: Market surveillance authorities approve testing in real-world conditions.

164.label: approves
164.description: Market surveillance authorities approve real-world testing plans.

165.label: supervises
165.description: Market surveillance authorities supervise testing in real-world conditions.

166.label: suspends
166.description: Market surveillance authorities can suspend testing in real-world conditions.

167.label: terminates
167.description: Market surveillance authorities can terminate testing in real-world conditions.

168.label: requiresModificationFrom
168.description: Market surveillance authorities can require providers to modify aspects of testing in real-world conditions.

169.label: requiresModificationFrom
169.description: Market surveillance authorities can require deployers to modify aspects of testing in real-world conditions.

170.label: organizesTestingFor
170.description: Market surveillance authorities organize testing of AI systems at the request of fundamental rights authorities.

171.label: grantedAccessBy
171.description: Market surveillance authorities are granted access to data and documentation by providers for compliance assessment.

172.label: supervises
172.description: Market surveillance authorities supervise AI systems used by financial institutions.

173.label: isDesignatedFor
173.description: Market surveillance authorities are designated for AI systems used for law enforcement, border management, and justice.

174.label: proposesJointActivitiesWith
174.description: Market surveillance authorities propose joint activities with the AI Office for compliance, identification of non-compliance, awareness, or guidance.

175.label: requestsInformationFrom
175.description: Market surveillance authorities may request information from the AI Office regarding general-purpose AI models for AI system investigation.

176.label: monitors
176.description: The AI Office monitors AI systems.

177.label: supervises
177.description: The AI Office supervises AI systems.

178.label: monitors
178.description: The AI Office monitors general-purpose AI models.

179.label: supervises
179.description: The AI Office supervises general-purpose AI models.

180.label: developsToolFor
180.description: The AI Office develops tools to facilitate deployers' compliance with obligations.

181.label: providesTemplateFor
181.description: The AI Office provides templates for summaries of training data for general-purpose AI models.

182.label: providesSupportFor
182.description: The AI Office provides coordination support for joint investigations by market surveillance authorities.

183.label: conductsEvaluationsOf
183.description: The AI Office conducts evaluations of general-purpose AI models to assess compliance.

184.label: investigates
184.description: The AI Office investigates systemic risks in systemic risk general-purpose AI models.

185.label: requestsAccessTo
185.description: The AI Office requests access to general-purpose AI models for evaluation purposes.

186.label: requestsMeasuresFrom
186.description: The AI Office requests providers to take measures for compliance or risk mitigation.

187.label: requests
187.description: The AI Office can request the withdrawal of AI systems based on general-purpose AI models.

188.label: requests
188.description: The AI Office can request the recall of AI systems based on general-purpose AI models.

189.label: alerts
189.description: The scientific panel may provide a qualified alert to the AI Office regarding possible systemic risks.

190.label: advisesOnClassificationOf
190.description: The scientific panel provides advice on the classification of systemic risk general-purpose AI models.

191.label: isIndependentOf
191.description: Experts on the scientific panel are independent of any provider of AI systems or general-purpose AI models.

192.label: carriedOutBy
192.description: Law enforcement activities are carried out by law enforcement authorities or on their behalf.

193.label: isA
193.description: Providers established in a third country whose AI system output is used in the Union are considered Union Market Operators (Third Country).

194.label: isA
194.description: Deployers established in a third country whose AI system output is used in the Union are considered Union Market Operators (Third Country).

195.label: uses
195.description: Union Market Operators (Third Country) use AI systems where the output is used in the Union.

196.label: incurs
196.description: Non-compliance with prohibited AI practices triggers administrative fines.

197.label: imposedOn
197.description: Administrative fines for general-purpose AI model providers are imposed on the provider.

198.label: assessesImpactOf
198.description: A fundamental rights impact assessment evaluates the potential impact of a high-risk AI system on fundamental rights.

199.label: presents
199.description: An AI system with a risk-presenting status presents risks to health, safety, or fundamental rights.

200.label: obtainedFrom
200.description: The right to AI explanation is obtained from the deployer.

201.label: concernsRoleOf
201.description: The right to AI explanation concerns the role of an AI system in decision-making.

202.label: allowsProcessingOf
202.description: The AI regulatory sandbox allows for the processing of personal data under specific conditions for AI system development.

203.label: facilitatesDevelopmentOf
203.description: The AI regulatory sandbox facilitates the development, training, testing, and validation of AI systems.

204.label: isGovernedBy
204.description: An AI regulatory sandbox operates pursuant to a specific sandbox plan.

205.label: mayInclude
205.description: AI regulatory sandboxes may include testing in real-world conditions.

206.label: providesPriorityAccessTo
206.description: AI regulatory sandboxes provide priority access to SMEs (providers) that fulfil eligibility conditions.

207.label: identifies
207.description: Competent authorities identify risks within the AI regulatory sandbox.

208.label: enablesEvaluationBy
208.description: The post-market monitoring system enables the provider to evaluate continuous compliance of AI systems.

209.label: collectsDataFrom
209.description: The post-market monitoring system collects data from deployers on AI system performance.

210.label: monitorsUseOf
210.description: The post-market monitoring system monitors the use of AI systems.

211.label: designates
211.description: The AI Office may designate a general-purpose AI model as presenting systemic risks.
